subject: Economics
chapter: Forms of Market and Price Determination

cases.passage: In a small town, there are two bakeries - 'Fresh Bites' and 'Sweet Delights'. Fresh Bites is a small family-owned business that has been operating for 10 years, while Sweet Delights is a new entrant in the market. Both bakeries offer a variety of bread and pastries at competitive prices. However, Fresh Bites has a loyal customer base due to its high-quality products and personalized service. Sweet Delights, on the other hand, has been offering discounts and promotions to attract new customers. The town has a population of 50,000, and the demand for bread and pastries is increasing due to the growing number of working professionals. The bakeries operate in a perfectly competitive market, with free entry and exit, and perfect information among buyers and sellers.

questions.q: What type of market structure do the bakeries operate in?
questions.explanation: The bakeries operate in a perfectly competitive market because there are many buyers and sellers, free entry and exit, and perfect information among buyers and sellers.

questions.q: What is the main reason for Sweet Delights' marketing strategy?
questions.explanation: Sweet Delights is using discounts and promotions to attract new customers and increase its market share.

questions.q: Why is Fresh Bites able to maintain a loyal customer base?
questions.explanation: Fresh Bites has a loyal customer base due to its high-quality products and personalized service.

questions.q: What is the impact of the growing number of working professionals on the demand for bread and pastries?
questions.explanation: The growing number of working professionals is increasing the demand for bread and pastries in the town.

questions.q: What is the characteristic of the market in the town?
questions.explanation: The market in the town has free entry and exit, which is a characteristic of a perfectly competitive market.

cases.passage: The Indian government has introduced a new policy to regulate the prices of essential commodities, including rice and wheat. The policy aims to ensure that these commodities are available at affordable prices to the poor and vulnerable sections of society. The government has fixed a minimum support price for these commodities, which is the price at which the farmers will be paid for their produce. The government has also established a buffer stock to regulate the supply of these commodities. However, some farmers are opposing the policy, arguing that it will lead to a decrease in their income.

questions.q: What is the main objective of the government's policy?
opts: To ensure affordable prices for essential commodities
To reduce the supply of rice and wheat
questions.explanation: The government's policy aims to ensure that essential commodities like rice and wheat are available at affordable prices to the poor and vulnerable sections of society.

questions.q: What is the role of the minimum support price in the policy?
opts: To regulate the supply of rice and wheat
To ensure that farmers are paid a fair price for their produce
To reduce the demand for rice and wheat
questions.explanation: The minimum support price ensures that farmers are paid a fair price for their produce, which is the price at which the farmers will be paid for their produce.

questions.q: Why are some farmers opposing the policy?
opts: Because they will have to pay a higher price for their produce
Because they will have to sell their produce at a lower price
Because it will lead to a decrease in their income
Because it will increase their income
questions.explanation: Some farmers are opposing the policy because it will lead to a decrease in their income.

questions.q: What is the role of the buffer stock in the policy?
opts: To regulate the supply of rice and wheat
To ensure that farmers are paid a fair price for their produce
To increase the demand for rice and wheat
To reduce the prices of rice and wheat
questions.explanation: The buffer stock is used to regulate the supply of rice and wheat, which helps to maintain stable prices in the market.

questions.q: What type of market control is the government using to regulate the prices of rice and wheat?
questions.explanation: The government is using a price floor to regulate the prices of rice and wheat, which is the minimum support price.
